title: Writing a formal letter of complaint
explanation:
Writing a formal letter of complaint

A formal complaint letter should be polite, precise and solution-focused.

  • State the reason for writing.
  • Give dates and facts.
  • Explain the impact.
  • Request a specific remedy.

questions.text: [*I am writing to complain about the poor service I received on 12 March.|I write complain poor service 12 March.|Complaint poor service March 12.]
[*Despite several reminders, the issue has not been resolved.|Despite reminders issue not resolved.|Issue despite reminders.]
[*As a result, I have been unable to use the product I paid for.|As result unable use product paid.|Result unable product.]
[*I would be grateful if you could offer a full refund.|Grateful full refund offer could.|Refund full grateful.]

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A formal letter should use polite but ____ language. | *firm | casual | humorous
Include exact dates and useful ____.  | *details | jokes | opinions
Avoid vague complaints; state the problem ____.  | *clearly | secretly | slowly
End with a professional ____. | *closing | recipe | headline
[*I look forward to hearing from you within fourteen days.|Look forward hearing fourteen days.|Forward look hearing days.]
[*I have enclosed copies of the receipt and correspondence.|Enclosed copies receipt correspondence.|Receipt copies enclosed.]
[*I trust this matter will be dealt with promptly.|Trust matter dealt promptly.|Matter trust promptly.]
